Marketers Gain Enhanced Precision Through Proxima's Collaboration with Microsoft Curate for Commerce
data.description:
Proxima announced that they are leveraging Microsoft Curate for Commerce to enable advertisers to target their social-first, first-party ecommerce transaction-based audiences across Microsoft Monetize supply. These audience deals will be offered exclusively via Microsoft Advertising and can be activated via advertisers' DSP of choice. With activation on Microsoft Curate for Commerce, marketers gain access to: Privacy-forward audience segments informed by deterministic commerce signals; Improved efficiency through superior targeting, creative delivery, and optimized placement; Incremental campaign performance powered by transactional data from real shopping outcomes.

Because marketers can activate in their DSP of choice, they can integrate this buy into their existing workflows, driving operational efficiency. Proxima is available for pilot activations with select advertisers and agencies. Brands and partners can contact Proxima to scope category coverage, onboarding pathways, and measurement plans.

data.title: Niron Magnetics and Stellantis Partner to Pioneer Rare-Earth-Free Electric Motor Innovation
data.description:
Niron Magnetics announced a project in collaboration with Stellantis to develop a new generation of electric motor designs for automotive use using magnets free of rare-earth elements. Niron's breakthrough Iron Nitride magnet technology offers a promising alternative to traditional rare-earth-based magnets, helping to reduce dependence on foreign-dominated supply chains. The collaboration builds upon Stellantis Ventures' strategic investment in Niron Magnetics in 2023.

It features additional support from the U.S. Department of Energy's Advanced Materials and Manufacturing Technologies Office (AMMTO), which contributed $2.7 million to the project as part of its strategy to strengthen American energy independence and secure critical materials. Permanent magnets play a vital role in dozens of vehicle systems, from audio and power steering to fluid pumps and powertrains. Magnets are critical components in a wide range of powertrains, including those used in internal combustion engines, hybrids, and electric vehicles.

Traditional permanent magnets rely heavily on rare earth elements, primarily sourced from China. In contrast, Niron Magnetics' Iron Nitride magnets are made from abundant iron and atmospheric nitrogen, materials readily available in the United States and globally. The Stellantis-Niron project aims to enhance motor architecture across all vehicle types by leveraging the unique properties of Iron Nitride magnets to deliver improved performance and efficiency.

The Stellantis- Niron project aims to enhance motor Architecture across all vehicle types by leveraging The unique properties of Iron Nitride magnet to deliver improved performance and efficiency". Titled " variable Flux Field Intensifying Motors Using Iron Nitride Permanent Magnets," the joint project builds on Stellantis' investment and ongoing collaboration with Niron Magnetics in areas such as traction motors, automotive audio, and other magnet-driven systems. The announcement coincides with the groundbreaking of Niron Magnetics' first commercial-scale manufacturing facility in Sartell, Minnesota. When fully operational, the site will be one of North America's largest magnet production facilities, producing 1,500 tons of magnets annually and creating 175 full-time jobs.

Niron Magnetics and Stellantis will also collaborate with leading research institutions, including Oak Ridge National Laboratory and the Illinois Institute of Technology, to leverage their expertise in advanced motor systems analysis and testing.

data.title: Artisight Introduces Smart Hospital Rooms with Ambient Documentation and Touchless Authentication
data.description:
Artisight announced a pilot to transform clinical workflows by extending Microsoft Dragon Copilot with the Artisight Smart Hospital Platform. Connecting Artisight and Dragon Copilot will bring ambient AI technology into the hospital room itself, creating a hands-free and device-free documentation interface for nurses. At the core of this capability is Artisight's ability to pair each nurse's unique voice profile with their real-time location system (RTLS) badge for two-factor authentication for security, eliminating the need for a device, manual logins or physical contact with shared workstations.

With this collaboration, documentation can happen in the background while nurses walk into the room and start caring for their patients. The pilot is currently underway at a leading hospital innovation center, with plans for a finalized extension with Microsoft Dragon Copilot following comprehensive clinical validation. During the initial pilot, nurses said they were able to document with higher frequency, and the AI-generated clinical summaries delivered a better, more accurate reflection of the care provided during the encounter.

Nurses also said the hands-free platform would better enable them to provide hands-on patient care and save time on documentation. Hospitals can expect measurable improvements in efficiency, accuracy, and compliance. This development builds on Artisight's mission to unify hospital operations through AI-powered computer vision, ambient sensors, and real-time communication.

Smart hospital rooms equipped with these capabilities showcase the future of healthcare delivery--where technology fades into the background and clinicians can dedicate their attention to what matters most: caring for patients.
